target: data/sections.json part1.html — "The 0.20 coefficient: fitted, not derived" subsection

defense_neutralization.original_defense: A dome defender could argue the review presents OLD table values as current, creating a strawman by mixing formula versions. The reviewer cites 3.49 and 28.8 as formula outputs when they are superseded table values.
defense_neutralization.neutralization_status: ALREADY PARTIALLY NEUTRALIZED — Part 1 formula paragraph was corrected in a prior cycle to cite formula values (1.83, 2.84, 21.53) and flag the table discrepancy with cross-reference to Section 2.9. Part 7 no longer contains the stale "28.8 at disc edge" claim. Remaining work: strengthen the "fitted, not derived" argument with coefficient change history and the anomalous n=1.40 finding.
defense_neutralization.residual_note: After this expansion, the only remaining defense is the generic "iterative refinement is good science" claim, which our text already addresses by noting the absence of sensitivity analysis and physical derivation.

replacement_html:
The 0.20 coefficient: fitted, not derived

The coefficient 0.20 in the formula has no physical derivation. The model page describes it as a “V13 optimization” — meaning it was adjusted to reduce distance errors. No property of the aetheric medium, no wave-propagation analysis, no optical experiment produces 0.20. It is a free parameter tuned to match known distances.

The dome’s own Height Table still lists values computed from an earlier coefficient (~0.27): n = 3.49 at the ice wall and n = 28.8 at r = 40,000 km, versus the current formula’s 2.84 and 21.53. The table was not updated when the coefficient changed — creating 19–31% internal discrepancies (Section 2.9). More revealing: the equatorial ring table value n = 1.40 requires a coefficient of ~0.097 — inconsistent with both the current 0.20 and the older ~0.27. This suggests the table was assembled piecemeal from multiple formula versions calibrated at different radii, each fit independently to match a particular route.

This matters because a free coefficient in a correction formula can always be adjusted to improve one measurement while degrading others. The dome’s own coordinate page shows this: the V13 system achieves 6.2% RMSE on cross-equatorial routes (using 0.20), but what happens if you change it to 0.25? Or 0.15? The page never reports a sensitivity analysis. Without one, we cannot know whether 0.20 is a unique optimum dictated by physics or an arbitrary choice that happens to minimize error on a particular set of calibration routes.
holes_addressed: Part 1 "fitted, not derived" section lacked specific evidence of the coefficient change (~0.27 to 0.20)
Equatorial ring n=1.40 anomaly not previously noted (matches neither coefficient)
Table-vs-formula discrepancy referenced but not exploited in the fitting argument
new_evidence_added: Reverse-engineered coefficient for n=1.40 at equatorial ring: c=0.097 (matches no known dome version)
Demonstrated that ice wall (c≈0.27) and equatorial ring (c≈0.097) table values require different coefficients — piecemeal fitting
Verified all n(r) values independently: c=0.20 gives 1.83/2.84/21.53; c=0.27 gives 2.12/3.48/28.71

anticipated_objections.objection: The table values are from an older version and have been superseded by the formula.
anticipated_objections.response: If superseded, why are they still published on the dome's coordinate page? And even granting this, the equatorial ring value n=1.40 requires c=0.097, which predates even the ~0.27 version — meaning the table contains values from at least THREE different formula versions, none of which the dome documents.

anticipated_objections.objection: Iterative coefficient refinement (0.27→0.20) is normal scientific practice.
anticipated_objections.response: Normal scientific refinement is driven by new physics or new data with documented methodology. The dome provides no physical motivation for 0.20 over 0.27, no error analysis, and no sensitivity study. Reducing a key parameter by 26% to improve route fits IS parameter fitting — the definition of what the dome claims it doesn't do.

anticipated_objections.objection: The 0.097 reverse-engineering is just arithmetic — it doesn't prove anything about the dome's history.
anticipated_objections.response: It proves the table value 1.40 is inconsistent with ANY single coefficient. The dome publishes one formula with one coefficient, yet its table requires at least two different coefficients to reproduce. This is not a matter of interpretation — it is a mathematical fact that can be verified by evaluating the formula.

kernel_of_truth: The dome model specifies an explicit, computable refraction formula n(r) with defined parameters — more rigorous than most alternative cosmologies. The coefficient reduction from 0.27 to 0.20 does show iterative refinement, and the formula is monotonically increasing from pole to rim, which is at least structurally plausible. Having a formula is better than hand-waving.

verification.note: All values computed using H(r) = 8537 × exp(-r/8619) km. Minor rounding differences between our stated values and computed values are within expected precision for the dome's published parameters.
